title: ballota nigra var. ruderalis
intro: Ballota nigra var. ruderalis, also known as common bugle or wild bugle, is a variety of flowering plant in the mint family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been shown to aid in the relief of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and menstrual cramps. The plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and promoting overall well-being. In addition to its medicinal uses, the plant has also been used in traditional folk medicine for its antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making it a valuable natural remedy for wound healing and skin infections.
